 {"id":"caam-2l9p.1","title":"Auth Detection System","description":"## Overview
Detect existing authentication files in standard locations for each supported provider.

## Problem Statement
Every new caam user already has existing auth credentials from using CLI tools directly:
- Claude users have ~/.claude.json or ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
- Codex users have ~/.codex/auth.json
- Gemini users have ~/.gemini/settings.json and oauth_credentials.json

Currently, these users must either log in again through caam or manually copy auth files. This creates friction in the critical first-use experience.

## Design Decisions

### Provider Interface Extension
**Decision:** Add `DetectExistingAuth()` method to Provider interface
**Rationale:** Each provider knows its own auth file locations and formats.

### Non-Destructive
**Decision:** Detection never modifies original files
**Rationale:** Users must explicitly choose to import. Detection is read-only.

### Return Metadata, Not Contents
**Decision:** Return location, timestamps, file size - not actual credentials
**Rationale:** Sufficient for user decision; actual reading happens during import.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. AuthDetection Struct
```go
// AuthDetection represents detected existing auth in the system
type AuthDetection struct {
    Provider    string            // e.g., "claude", "codex", "gemini"
    Found       bool              // Whether any auth was detected
    Locations   []AuthLocation    // All detected auth file locations
    Primary     *AuthLocation     // Recommended/most recent location
    Warning     string            // Any issues (e.g., "multiple auth files found")
}

type AuthLocation struct {
    Path         string    // Absolute path to auth file
    Exists       bool      // File exists
    LastModified time.Time // Modification time
    FileSize     int64     // Size in bytes
    IsValid      bool      // Basic format validation passed
    ValidationError string // If IsValid=false, why
}
```

### 2. Provider Interface Addition
```go
type Provider interface {
    // ... existing methods ...
    DetectExistingAuth() (*AuthDetection, error)
}
```

### 3. Claude Provider Implementation
Locations to check:
- ~/.claude.json (legacy)
- ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json (current)
- ~/.config/anthropic/credentials.json (API key fallback)

### 4. Codex Provider Implementation
Locations to check:
- ~/.codex/auth.json
- ~/.config/codex/credentials (if exists)

### 5. Gemini Provider Implementation
Locations to check:
- ~/.gemini/settings.json
- ~/.gemini/oauth_credentials.json
- ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json

### 6. CLI: Create `caam auth detect` Command
```
caam auth detect [tool]

# Output example:
Claude:
  ✓ ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
    Last modified: 2024-12-15 10:30:00
    Size: 1.2 KB
    Status: Valid OAuth token

Codex:
  ✓ ~/.codex/auth.json
    Last modified: 2024-12-10 14:22:00
    Size: 0.8 KB
    Status: Valid API key

Gemini:
  ✗ No existing auth detected

Run 'caam auth import <tool>' to import detected credentials.
```

### 7. Basic Validation
Without making API calls:
- JSON parseable?
- Expected fields present?
- Token format looks valid?

## Testing Strategy
- Mock filesystem with various auth file scenarios
- Test each provider's detection logic
- Test missing files, corrupt files, partial configs
- Test detection command output formatting

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/provider/provider.go - Add interface method
- internal/provider/claude.go - Implement detection
- internal/provider/codex.go - Implement detection
- internal/provider/gemini.go - Implement detection
- cmd/caam/cmd/auth.go - Add detect command

## Success Criteria
`caam auth detect` accurately shows all existing auth files with useful metadata.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:35:10.408718934-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:54:09.653960175-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:54:09.653965946-05:00","close_reason":"Auth Detection System implemented: DetectExistingAuth() in all providers, caam auth detect CLI command with --json support. Fixed duplicate truncateDescription function in root.go. All tests passing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-2l9p.1","depends_on_id":"caam-2l9p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:35:10.409921796-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-2l9p.2","title":"Auth Import Command","description":"## Overview
Import existing auth credentials into a new caam profile.

## Problem Statement
Users who have existing auth want to use it with caam without re-authenticating. The `caam auth detect` feature identifies existing auth; this feature provides the mechanism to import it.

## Design Decisions

### Standalone Command
**Decision:** `caam auth import` as standalone command (not just wizard feature)
**Rationale:** Users who skip the init wizard or add profiles later need direct access.

### Default Profile Name
**Decision:** Default to "default" or "imported" if not specified
**Rationale:** Reasonable default; user can rename later.

### Copy, Not Move
**Decision:** Copy auth files into caam vault, don't move originals
**Rationale:** 
1. Original tools still work if user runs them directly
2. Non-destructive - user can delete originals later if desired
3. Matches user expectations (import = copy)

### Isolation by Default
**Decision:** Imported auth goes into isolated profile directory
**Rationale:** Core caam value prop - profile isolation. Imported auth becomes isolated.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. CLI: Create `caam auth import` Command
```
caam auth import <tool> [flags]

Flags:
  --name, -n        Profile name (default: "default")
  --description, -d Profile description
  --force           Overwrite if profile exists
  --source          Path to auth file (override detection)
```

### 2. Import Flow
```
1. Call provider.DetectExistingAuth()
2. If no auth found and no --source, error
3. If --source provided, validate it exists
4. Check if target profile name exists (error if exists, no --force)
5. Create new profile with vault.CreateProfile()
6. Copy auth files to profile's directories
7. Set up passthrough symlinks
8. Validate imported auth (basic check)
9. Report success with next steps
```

### 3. Provider Interface Addition
```go
type Provider interface {
    // ... existing methods ...
    ImportAuth(detection *AuthDetection, targetDir string) error
}
```

### 4. Success Output
```
✓ Imported Claude auth to profile 'work'

Profile location: ~/.local/share/caam/vault/claude/work/
Auth files copied:
  - auth.json (OAuth token)

Next steps:
  1. Activate profile: caam activate claude work
  2. Verify it works: caam doctor
  3. (Optional) Delete original: rm ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
```

### 5. Multi-Source Handling
If detection finds multiple auth locations:
```
Multiple Claude auth sources detected:
  [1] ~/.claude.json (modified 2024-12-15)
  [2] ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json (modified 2024-12-18)

Use --source to specify which to import, or we'll use the most recent.
```

## Dependencies
- **caam-2l9p.1 (Auth Detection):** Import needs detection to find auth files.

## Edge Cases
- No auth detected and no --source → Helpful error with guidance
- Multiple auth sources → Use most recent, or prompt
- Target profile exists → Error unless --force
- Auth file is invalid → Warn but import anyway (let doctor catch issues)
- Source file permissions → Handle gracefully

## Testing Strategy
- Import with detection (happy path)
- Import with --source override
- Import to existing profile (error)
- Import to existing profile with --force
- Import when no auth exists

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/cmd/auth.go - Add import command
- internal/provider/provider.go - Add ImportAuth interface method
- internal/provider/claude.go - Implement ImportAuth
- internal/provider/codex.go - Implement ImportAuth
- internal/provider/gemini.go - Implement ImportAuth
- internal/vault/vault.go - May need helper methods

## Success Criteria
New user can run `caam auth import claude` and have a working profile in seconds.","notes":"Implemented caam auth import command with ImportAuth provider interface method for all 3 providers (claude, codex, gemini)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:35:43.040672608-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:53:05.586068272-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:53:05.58607773-05:00","close_reason":"Fully implemented caam auth import command with provider ImportAuth interface","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-2l9p.2","depends_on_id":"caam-2l9p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:35:43.041857857-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-2l9p.2","depends_on_id":"caam-2l9p.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:35:43.043156821-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-2l9p.3","title":"Init Wizard Auth Integration","description":"## Overview
Integrate auth detection and import into the existing init wizard.

## Problem Statement
The init wizard (`caam init`) already exists and works well, but it doesn't detect existing auth credentials. This is a missed opportunity for a seamless first-run experience.

## Design Approach

### Enhancement, Not Replacement
**Decision:** Add detection step to existing wizard flow
**Rationale:** The wizard is already good. We're enhancing, not rewriting.

### Flow Addition
After provider selection, before manual auth setup:
```
$ caam init

Welcome to caam!

Which AI coding tools do you use?
  [x] Claude CLI
  [ ] Codex CLI
  [ ] Gemini CLI

[NEW STEP]
✓ Detected existing Claude auth:
  ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
  Last modified: 2024-12-18 10:30:00
  
  Import this as your default profile? [Y/n] y
  
✓ Imported as profile 'default'

Would you like to add another profile? [y/N]
```

### Multiple Providers
If user selects multiple tools, check each and offer import for each.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Add Detection Step to init.go
After tool selection, call `provider.DetectExistingAuth()` for each selected tool.

### 2. Display Detection Results
Show what was found with modification timestamps. Use clear formatting.

### 3. Import Confirmation
Prompt user to confirm import. Default to 'yes' (most common choice).

### 4. Allow Custom Profile Name
If importing, optionally ask for profile name (default: "default").

### 5. Continue Normal Flow
After import, continue with existing wizard flow (passthrough config, etc.).

## Dependencies
- **caam-2l9p.2 (Auth Import):** Uses the same underlying import logic.

## User Experience

### Happy Path
```
$ caam init
Welcome to caam!

Which tools? > [x] Claude, [x] Codex

Checking for existing auth...
✓ Claude: ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json (modified 3 days ago)
✓ Codex: ~/.codex/auth.json (modified 1 week ago)

Import detected credentials?
  [x] Claude → profile 'default'
  [x] Codex → profile 'default'
  [Import] [Skip]

✓ Imported 2 profiles

You're all set! Try:
  caam ls              # List your profiles
  caam activate claude # Use Claude
  claude               # Run Claude CLI
```

### No Auth Found
If no auth detected, wizard continues normally with manual setup guidance.

## Testing Strategy
- Test wizard with detected auth (import path)
- Test wizard with no auth (normal path)
- Test partial detection (some providers have auth, others don't)
- Test user decline import

## Files to Modify
- cmd/caam/cmd/init.go - Add detection and import steps

## Success Criteria
New user with existing auth can run `caam init`, confirm import, and have working profiles in under 60 seconds.","notes":"Implementation complete. Auth detection (detectProviderAuth) and import (importDetectedAuth) are integrated into the init wizard. Build is temporarily blocked by caam-u335.1 adding ValidateToken to the provider interface.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:37:06.567837008-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:01:02.271032633-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T00:01:02.27103652-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-2l9p.3","depends_on_id":"caam-2l9p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:37:06.56897581-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-2l9p.3","depends_on_id":"caam-2l9p.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:37:06.570035984-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"caam-33mv","title":"Implement caam precheck command","description":"# Task: Implement caam precheck command

## Overview
Create the comprehensive precheck command that shows session planning with recommended profiles, backup options, burn rates, and rotation forecast.

## Technical Details

### Command
```go
var precheckCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "precheck [provider]",
    Short: "Plan your session - see which profile is best",
    RunE: runPrecheck,
}

func runPrecheck(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // 1. Fetch all profiles for provider
    // 2. Get real-time rate limits
    // 3. Get burn rates from logs
    // 4. Get identity info
    // 5. Check cooldowns
    // 6. Check auth pool status
    // 7. Generate recommendations
    // 8. Calculate rotation forecast
    // 9. Render report
}
```

### Output Format
Rich ASCII visualization showing:
- Recommended profile with usage bars
- Backup profiles
- Profiles in cooldown
- Today consumption summary
- Forecast with rotation plan

## Dependencies
- Implement depletion prediction engine
- Implement pre-rotation alert system
- Create internal/logs package
- Create AuthPool struct and manager","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:19:47.946116152-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:11:53.546078282-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-33mv","depends_on_id":"caam-t1p8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:48.290419869-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-33mv","depends_on_id":"caam-hort","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:48.319787238-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-33mv","depends_on_id":"caam-xz27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:48.3516339-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-33mv","depends_on_id":"caam-93ud","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:48.380051228-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-37az","title":"[FEATURE] TUI Open Account Page: Implement 'w' key to open provider account page in browser","description":"BACKGROUND: TUI has 'w' key bound to 'open account webpage' but handler just says 'not yet implemented' (model.go:1075).

PROVIDER ACCOUNT URLS:
- claude: https://console.anthropic.com/
- codex: https://platform.openai.com/
- gemini: https://aistudio.google.com/

KEY FILES:
- internal/tui/model.go line 1075 (handleOpenAccountPage stub)

APPROACH:
1. Create provider → URL mapping (could be in authfile/providers.go or new file)
2. Implement cross-platform browser launch:
   - Linux: xdg-open
   - macOS: open
   - Windows: start
   - Fallback: print URL and ask user to open manually
3. Wire to TUI action handler
4. Handle edge cases (SSH session, no display)

CROSS-PLATFORM BROWSER LAUNCH OPTIONS:
Option A: Use exec.Command with runtime.GOOS detection
Option B: Use github.com/pkg/browser package (adds dependency)
Decision: Option A - keep dependencies minimal, code is simple

EDGE CASES:
- SSH session with no DISPLAY: detect and show URL in status instead
- WSL: may need special handling
- Headless servers: graceful fallback

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Pressing 'w' opens provider account page in default browser
- Works on Linux, macOS, Windows
- Graceful fallback for headless/SSH

RATIONALE: Convenience feature. When you need to check your subscription status or regenerate API key, quick access to account page saves time. Simple implementation.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:19:49.007716816-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T21:56:38.591902953-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T21:56:38.591902953-05:00","close_reason":"TUI 'open in browser' feature fully implemented: provider URLs in internal/provider/provider.go, cross-platform browser launch in internal/browser/browser.go, wired to 'o' key in model.go:727 handleOpenInBrowser()","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-37az","depends_on_id":"caam-9isk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:27:53.168154326-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-37az","depends_on_id":"caam-x37s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:27:58.276085725-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-37az","depends_on_id":"caam-pa7b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:28:03.38622187-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-39a","title":"Add sync management to TUI","description":"# Task: Add sync management to TUI

## Context
Expose sync functionality in the TUI for users who prefer the graphical interface. This includes viewing sync status, managing machines, and triggering syncs.

## TUI Changes

### New Panel: Sync Status
Add a new panel/view accessible via 'S' key:

```
┌─ Sync Pool ─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Status: Enabled (auto-sync on)                              │
│ Last sync: 5 minutes ago                                    │
│                                                             │
│ Machines:                                                   │
│   🟢 work-laptop     192.168.1.100     synced 5m ago       │
│   🟢 home-desktop    10.0.0.50         synced 5m ago       │
│   🔴 cloud-vm        34.123.45.67      offline (2h)        │
│                                                             │
│ Pending: 0 | Failed: 1 | Queue: 1 item                     │
│                                                             │
│ [a]dd  [r]emove  [t]est  [s]ync now  [e]dit CSV  [q]uit   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Status Indicators
- 🟢 Online, synced
- 🟡 Online, pending sync
- 🔴 Offline or error
- 🔄 Currently syncing

### Keyboard Shortcuts (in sync view)
- 'a' - Add machine (opens dialog)
- 'r' - Remove selected machine (with confirmation)
- 't' - Test selected machine connectivity
- 's' - Sync now with all/selected machine
- 'e' - Edit CSV file in editor
- 'l' - View sync log
- 'Enter' - View machine details
- 'Esc/q' - Return to main view

### Add Machine Dialog
When 'a' is pressed:
```
┌─ Add Machine to Sync Pool ─────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│ Name:     [work-laptop________________]                     │
│ Address:  [192.168.1.100______________]                     │
│ SSH Key:  [~/.ssh/id_ed25519__________]                     │
│                                                             │
│           [Add] [Test & Add] [Cancel]                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Machine Details View
When Enter is pressed on a machine:
```
┌─ Machine: work-laptop ──────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│ Address:      192.168.1.100                                 │
│ SSH User:     jeff                                          │
│ SSH Key:      ~/.ssh/id_ed25519                             │
│ Remote Path:  ~/.local/share/caam                           │
│ Status:       Online (latency: 23ms)                        │
│ Added:        2025-12-15 10:30:00                           │
│ Last Sync:    2025-12-19 14:24:00 (5 min ago)              │
│                                                             │
│ Profiles synced: 8                                          │
│   claude: 5 profiles                                        │
│   codex: 2 profiles                                         │
│   gemini: 1 profile                                         │
│                                                             │
│ Recent sync activity:                                       │
│   14:24:00  claude/alice@gmail.com    pushed    ✓          │
│   14:24:01  codex/work@company.com    pulled    ✓          │
│                                                             │
│           [Test] [Sync] [Remove] [Back]                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Sync Progress Overlay
When sync is running:
```
┌─ Syncing... ────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│ work-laptop:   [████████████████░░░░] 80%                  │
│   ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com (pushed)                        │
│   ✓ claude/bob@gmail.com (up to date)                      │
│   → codex/work@company.com (syncing...)                    │
│                                                             │
│ home-desktop:  [████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 40%                  │
│   ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com (pushed)                        │
│   ...                                                       │
│                                                             │
│                        [Cancel]                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Integration with Main View
In the main profile list, show sync status:
```
claude
  alice@gmail.com     active    🔄 syncing
  bob@gmail.com                 🟢 synced
  carol@gmail.com               ⚠️ pending
```

### Help Panel Update
Add sync shortcuts to help:
```
Sync:
  S       Open sync panel
  Ctrl+S  Sync now (from any view)
```

## Implementation

### New Files
- `internal/tui/sync_view.go` - Sync panel view
- `internal/tui/sync_model.go` - Sync panel model
- `internal/tui/sync_update.go` - Sync panel update handlers

### Model Changes
```go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    syncView    *SyncViewModel
    showingSync bool
}

type SyncViewModel struct {
    pool          *sync.SyncPool
    machines      []*sync.Machine
    selectedIdx   int
    syncing       bool
    syncProgress  map[string]float64
    lastRefresh   time.Time
}
```

### Key Handling
In `update.go`:
```go
case "S":
    return m.showSyncView()
    
case "ctrl+s":
    return m.triggerSyncNow()
```

### Sync Panel
```go
func (m SyncViewModel) View() string {
    // Render sync panel using lipgloss
    // - Header with status
    // - Machine list with status indicators
    // - Footer with keybindings
}

func (m SyncViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (SyncViewModel, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case tea.KeyMsg:
        switch msg.String() {
        case "a":
            return m.showAddMachineDialog()
        case "r":
            return m.confirmRemoveMachine()
        // etc
        }
    case SyncProgressMsg:
        // Update progress display
    case SyncCompleteMsg:
        // Show results, refresh
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'S' key opens sync panel
- [ ] Sync panel shows machine list with status
- [ ] Add machine dialog works
- [ ] Remove machine with confirmation works
- [ ] Test connectivity from TUI works
- [ ] Sync now from TUI works
- [ ] Sync progress overlay shows during sync
- [ ] Machine details view shows full info
- [ ] Sync log accessible from TUI
- [ ] Edit CSV opens editor
- [ ] Help text updated
- [ ] Ctrl+S triggers sync from any view
- [ ] Proper error handling and display

## Files to Create
- `internal/tui/sync_view.go`
- `internal/tui/sync_model.go`
- `internal/tui/sync_update.go`

## Files to Modify
- `internal/tui/model.go` - Add sync state
- `internal/tui/update.go` - Add key handlers
- `internal/tui/view.go` - Add sync panel rendering
- `internal/tui/help.go` - Update help text

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-i5i (text input dialog - for add machine)
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)

 {"id":"caam-3am","title":"Smart Profile Management (codex-pool inspired)","description":"# Smart Profile Management Epic

## Origin & Inspiration
This epic brings intelligence from [codex-pool](https://github.com/darvell/codex-pool) to caam. codex-pool is a sophisticated proxy that load-balances requests across multiple AI accounts with automatic failover, token refresh, and usage tracking.

**Key Insight**: While codex-pool answers "which account for THIS request?", caam answers "which account for my WORK SESSION?" The intelligence transfers, but the application differs.

## Goals
1. **Prevent auth failures** through proactive token refresh
2. **Surface profile health** so users can make informed choices
3. **Track activity** for visibility into usage patterns
4. **Reduce friction** with hot reload and project associations

## Non-Goals (for now)
- Proxy mode (request interception)
- Automatic profile switching mid-session
- Rate limit detection from API responses

## Success Metrics
- Zero unexpected auth failures due to token expiry
- Users can identify problematic profiles at a glance
- Usage patterns are visible via dashboard
- TUI updates without manual refresh

## Design Document
See `docs/SMART_PROFILE_MANAGEMENT.md` for detailed design rationale, architecture decisions, and risk analysis.

## Sub-Epics
1. **Profile Health Foundation** - Core infrastructure
2. **Proactive Token Refresh** - Automatic token maintenance
3. **Usage Analytics** - Activity tracking and dashboard
4. **Runtime Enhancements** - Hot reload, signals
5. **Project Context** - Directory-profile associations

## Technical Approach
- SQLite for persistent storage (usage.db)
- JSON for lightweight metadata (health.json)
- Provider-specific OAuth handlers
- fsnotify for file watching","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:15:04.594984626-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:41:13.771976385-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:41:13.771976385-05:00","close_reason":"Planning complete: Design doc created at docs/SMART_PROFILE_MANAGEMENT.md. Implementation continues via sub-epics (caam-x0g, caam-e36, etc.)"}

 {"id":"caam-7vg","title":"Implement TUI backup action","description":"# Task: Implement TUI backup action

## Context
Wire up the backup functionality to the TUI using the new text input dialog component.

## Implementation

### Trigger
- Press 'b' or 'B' when viewing a provider's profile list
- Only available when auth files exist for that provider

### Flow
1. User presses 'b' on a provider
2. Check if auth files exist for provider
   - If not: show error message "No auth files found for <provider>"
   - If yes: continue
3. Open text input dialog
   - Title: "Backup Current Auth"
   - Prompt: "Enter profile name (e.g., email address):"
   - Placeholder: "user@example.com"
4. User enters profile name, presses Enter
5. Validate input:
   - Not empty
   - No forbidden characters (/, \, etc.)
   - Warn if profile already exists (overwrite?)
6. Call vault.Backup(provider, profileName)
7. Handle result:
   - Success: show message "Backed up <provider> auth to '<profile>'"
   - Error: show error message
8. Refresh profile list

### Overwrite Confirmation
If profile already exists, show confirmation dialog:
- "Profile '<name>' already exists. Overwrite? (y/n)"
- Or use the dialog with Yes/No buttons

### Key Bindings
- Add 'b' to the help text
- Document in keyboard shortcuts section

## Code Changes

### internal/tui/update.go
```go
case "b", "B":
    if m.canBackup() {
        return m.showBackupDialog()
    }
```

### internal/tui/model.go
Add method:
```go
func (m Model) showBackupDialog() (Model, tea.Cmd) {
    // Open dialog for backup
}

func (m Model) executeBackup(profileName string) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        err := m.vault.Backup(m.selectedProvider, profileName)
        // Return result message
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'b' key triggers backup dialog when on provider
- [ ] Dialog prompts for profile name
- [ ] Validation prevents empty/invalid names
- [ ] Existing profile triggers overwrite confirmation
- [ ] Successful backup shows confirmation message
- [ ] Failed backup shows error message
- [ ] Profile list refreshes after backup
- [ ] Help text updated with new keybinding

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-i5i (text input dialog component)
- Part of epic: caam-bvd (TUI Backup Feature)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:39:40.659826144-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:42:24.357463036-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:42:24.357463036-05:00","close_reason":"TUI backup action fully implemented with dialog components, key binding, validation, and overwrite confirmation. Tests fixed and passing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-7vg","depends_on_id":"caam-i5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:46:27.095207044-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"caam-82c","title":"Add force-unlock command","description":"## Task
Add 'caam unlock' command to forcibly remove locks.

## Usage
```bash
caam unlock codex work-1          # Unlock specific profile
caam unlock --stale               # Clean all stale locks
caam unlock codex work-1 --force  # Force unlock even if process running
```

## Safety
- Default: Only unlock if process not running
- --force: Unlock even if process appears running (dangerous)
- Warn before force unlock
- Consider sending SIGTERM to process before unlocking

## Implementation
- Check if lock is stale (PID not running)
- If stale, just remove lock file
- If --force, warn and remove anyway
- If not stale and no --force, error

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can unlock stale locks
- Force requires explicit flag
- Warning before dangerous operations","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.864750655-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:34:16.981893529-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam profile unlock command with stale lock detection and --force flag","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pxg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.879518279-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-82c","depends_on_id":"caam-pb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:39.880805974-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"caam-83za","title":"Integration tests: AuthPool + SmartRunner + Prediction","description":"# Task: Integration tests: AuthPool + SmartRunner + Prediction

## Overview
Integration tests that verify the interaction between major components works correctly end-to-end.

## Why This Matters
Unit tests verify individual components. But bugs often hide in the INTEGRATION:
- AuthPool provides ready profiles to SmartRunner
- Prediction engine triggers alerts that feed into SmartRunner decisions
- Session tracker feeds burn rate to prediction engine

## Test Scenarios

### 1. AuthPool → SmartRunner Integration
```go
func TestAuthPoolSmartRunnerIntegration(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing AuthPool provides ready profiles to SmartRunner")
    
    // Setup
    pool := authpool.NewTestPool(t)
    pool.AddProfile("claude", "alice", authpool.Ready)
    pool.AddProfile("claude", "bob", authpool.Ready)
    
    runner := exec.NewSmartRunner(
        exec.WithAuthPool(pool),
    )
    
    // Simulate rate limit
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Simulating rate limit on alice")
    err := runner.SimulateRateLimit()
    
    // Verify handoff to bob
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Current profile: %s", runner.CurrentProfile())
    require.Equal(t, "bob", runner.CurrentProfile())
    
    // Verify alice marked as cooldown in pool
    status := pool.GetStatus("claude", "alice")
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Alice pool status: %v", status)
    require.Equal(t, authpool.Cooldown, status)
}
```

### 2. Prediction → Alert → SmartRunner Integration
```go
func TestPredictionAlertIntegration(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing prediction triggers proactive handoff")
    
    // Setup prediction engine with mock data showing imminent depletion
    predictor := prediction.NewTestEngine(t)
    predictor.SetPrediction("alice", &prediction.Prediction{
        CurrentPercent: 92,
        TimeToDepletion: 5 * time.Minute,
        Warning: prediction.WarningImminent,
    })
    
    alertChan := make(chan *notify.Alert, 10)
    runner := exec.NewSmartRunner(
        exec.WithPredictor(predictor),
        exec.WithAlertCallback(func(a *notify.Alert) {
            alertChan <- a
        }),
    )
    
    // Start runner
    runner.Start(ctx)
    
    // Verify alert generated
    select {
    case alert := <-alertChan:
        t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Alert received: %+v", alert)
        require.Contains(t, alert.Message, "5 minutes")
    case <-time.After(time.Second):
        t.Fatal("Expected alert not received")
    }
}
```

### 3. SessionTracker → BurnRate → Prediction Integration
```go
func TestSessionTrackerPredictionIntegration(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing live session data feeds prediction")
    
    tracker := usage.NewSessionTracker()
    predictor := prediction.NewEngine(
        prediction.WithSessionTracker(tracker),
    )
    
    // Simulate usage
    for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
        tracker.Record(usage.TokenEntry{
            Timestamp:    time.Now().Add(-time.Duration(i) * time.Minute),
            OutputTokens: 1000,
        })
    }
    
    // Get prediction
    pred, err := predictor.Predict(ctx, "claude", "alice")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Burn rate: %.2f tokens/min", pred.BurnRate.TokensPerMinute)
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Time to depletion: %v", pred.TimeToDepletion)
    
    // Verify burn rate is approximately 1000 tokens/min
    require.InDelta(t, 1000, pred.BurnRate.TokensPerMinute, 100)
}
```

### 4. Vault → Pool → Handoff → Vault Integration
```go
func TestVaultPoolHandoffCycle(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[INTEGRATION] Testing full vault → pool → handoff cycle")
    
    // Setup real vault with test profiles
    vault := authfile.NewTestVault(t)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "alice", validAuthData)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "bob", validAuthData)
    
    pool := authpool.NewAuthPool(authpool.WithVault(vault))
    pool.MonitorOnce(ctx) // Populate pool state
    
    runner := exec.NewSmartRunner(
        exec.WithVault(vault),
        exec.WithAuthPool(pool),
    )
    
    // Verify initial state
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] Initial profile: %s", vault.ActiveProfile("claude"))
    
    // Trigger handoff
    runner.SimulateRateLimit()
    
    // Verify vault state changed
    t.Logf("[INTEGRATION] After handoff: %s", vault.ActiveProfile("claude"))
    require.NotEqual(t, "alice", vault.ActiveProfile("claude"))
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Every test prefixed with [INTEGRATION]
- Log state before and after each operation
- Log timing of operations
- Log all component interactions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All integration paths tested
- [ ] No mocking of integration points (use real components)
- [ ] Clear logging of component interactions
- [ ] Tests run in <10 seconds
- [ ] Deterministic (no flaky tests)

## Dependencies
- Implement SmartRunner with auto-handoff (caam-b6hj)
- Create AuthPool struct and manager (caam-93ud)
- Implement depletion prediction engine (caam-t1p8)
- Implement real-time session tracking (caam-blxo)

## Files to Create
- internal/exec/integration_test.go
- internal/authpool/integration_test.go
- internal/prediction/integration_test.go

 {"id":"caam-84lq","title":"Implement provider-specific login handlers","description":"# Task: Implement provider-specific login handlers

## Overview
Create login handlers for each provider that know how to trigger and complete the login flow via PTY injection.

## Background
Different providers have different login flows:
- Claude Code: /login → browser auth or device code
- Codex: Already running, may need codex login
- Gemini: /auth command

Each handler needs to know:
1. What command triggers login
2. What output indicates login in progress
3. What output indicates success
4. What output indicates failure

## Technical Details

### LoginHandler Interface
```go
// internal/handoff/handler.go

type LoginHandler interface {
    // Provider returns the provider ID this handler supports
    Provider() string
    
    // TriggerLogin injects the login command
    TriggerLogin(pty *pty.Controller) error
    
    // IsLoginInProgress returns true if login flow has started
    IsLoginInProgress(output string) bool
    
    // IsLoginComplete returns true if login succeeded
    IsLoginComplete(output string) bool
    
    // IsLoginFailed returns true if login failed, with error message
    IsLoginFailed(output string) (bool, string)
}
```

### Claude Handler
```go
type ClaudeLoginHandler struct{}

func (h *ClaudeLoginHandler) TriggerLogin(pty *pty.Controller) error {
    return pty.InjectCommand("/login")
}

func (h *ClaudeLoginHandler) IsLoginComplete(output string) bool {
    // Look for "Successfully logged in" or similar
    return strings.Contains(output, "logged in") ||
           strings.Contains(output, "authenticated")
}
```

### Handler Registry
```go
var handlers = map[string]LoginHandler{
    "claude": &ClaudeLoginHandler{},
    "codex":  &CodexLoginHandler{},
    "gemini": &GeminiLoginHandler{},
}

func GetHandler(provider string) LoginHandler {
    return handlers[provider]
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/handoff/handler.go
- internal/handoff/claude.go
- internal/handoff/codex.go
- internal/handoff/gemini.go

## Dependencies
- Create PTY controller for command injection","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:18:56.774963737-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:28:08.033224616-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:28:08.033224616-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Implemented LoginHandler interface with Registry, plus ClaudeLoginHandler, CodexLoginHandler, and GeminiLoginHandler. Each handler supports login command injection, progress detection, success detection, and failure detection with error messages. 12 tests passing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-84lq","depends_on_id":"caam-iz4s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:38.359796689-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-8d1v","title":"[EPIC] TUI Health Data Integration: Replace hardcoded health values with real data from health.Storage","description":"BACKGROUND: In model.go lines 1201-1238, profile health data is hardcoded:
  HealthStatus: health.StatusUnknown  // TODO: get from health store
  ErrorCount: 0                       // TODO: get from health store
  Penalty: 0                          // TODO: get from health store

The health.Storage API exists with GetProfile(), GetStatus(), ListProfiles() - just needs to be called.

KEY FILES:
- internal/tui/model.go lines 1190-1241 (buildProviderProfiles, syncDetailPanel)
- internal/health/storage.go (Storage struct with GetProfile, GetStatus)
- internal/health/status.go (CalculateStatus, status constants)

APPROACH:
1. Add health.Storage to Model struct (init in New())
2. In buildProviderProfiles(), call health.GetProfile() for each profile
3. In syncDetailPanel(), fetch current health data
4. Handle missing health data gracefully (profile never used = unknown status)

CONSIDERATIONS:
- Performance: batch-load all health data or lazy-load per profile?
  Decision: Load per-provider during buildProviderProfiles (bounded by # profiles)
- Staleness: health data could change while TUI is open
  Decision: v1 loads on startup, can add refresh timer later
- Should show 'new' badge for profiles with no health data?

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Profile list shows real health indicators (green/yellow/red)
- Detail panel shows real error count and penalty
- Missing health data shows 'unknown' status gracefully

 {"id":"caam-8y9","title":"Implement CLI sync commands","description":"# Task: Implement CLI sync commands

## Context
Implement the CLI commands for managing the sync pool and triggering synchronization.

## Commands

### `caam sync`
Sync now with all machines in pool.

```bash
caam sync [flags]

Flags:
  --machine NAME    Sync only with specific machine
  --provider PROV   Sync only specific provider
  --profile PROF    Sync only specific profile
  --dry-run         Show what would sync without doing it
  --force           Force sync even if recently synced
  --json            Output results as JSON
```

Output:
```
Syncing with 3 machines...

  work-laptop (192.168.1.100):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed (local fresher)
    ✓ claude/bob@gmail.com: pulled (remote fresher)
    ✓ codex/work@company.com: up to date
    
  home-desktop (10.0.0.50):
    ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com: pushed
    ✓ gemini/personal@gmail.com: pulled
    
  cloud-vm (34.123.45.67):
    ✗ Connection failed: timeout

Sync complete: 4 pushed, 2 pulled, 1 up to date, 1 error
```

### `caam sync init` ⭐ NEW
Interactive first-time setup wizard:

```bash
caam sync init [flags]

Flags:
  --discover    Auto-discover from SSH config
  --csv         Create CSV template only
```

Flow:
```
$ caam sync init

Welcome to CAAM Sync Setup!

This will help you set up syncing between your machines.

Step 1: Create your sync pool

  Discovered hosts from ~/.ssh/config:
    [1] work-laptop (192.168.1.100)
    [2] home-desktop (10.0.0.50)
    [3] cloud-vm (34.123.45.67)
    [a] Add all discovered hosts
    [m] Manually add a machine
    [s] Skip for now

  Choice: a

Step 2: Test connectivity

  Testing work-laptop... ✓ OK (23ms)
  Testing home-desktop... ✓ OK (45ms)  
  Testing cloud-vm... ✗ Failed: connection refused

  1 machine failed. Continue anyway? (Y/n): y

Step 3: Enable auto-sync?

  Auto-sync automatically pushes fresh tokens when you backup or refresh.
  
  Enable auto-sync? (y/N): y

Setup complete!
  Machines in pool: 3 (2 online, 1 offline)
  Auto-sync: enabled

Run 'caam sync' to sync now, or it will happen automatically.
```

### `caam sync status`
Show sync pool status and last sync times.

```bash
caam sync status [flags]

Flags:
  --json    Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync Status

Local Machine: jeffs-macbook
Auto-sync: enabled
Last full sync: 2025-12-19 14:29:00

Machines in pool:
  NAME           ADDRESS          STATUS    LAST SYNC
  work-laptop    192.168.1.100    🟢 online  5 min ago
  home-desktop   10.0.0.50        🟢 online  5 min ago
  cloud-vm       34.123.45.67     🔴 offline 2 hours ago (timeout)

Profiles tracked: 8
  claude: 5 profiles
  codex: 2 profiles
  gemini: 1 profile

Queue: 0 pending | History: 127 entries
```

### `caam sync add`
Add a machine to the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync add <name> <address> [flags]

Flags:
  --key PATH        Path to SSH private key (default: auto-detect)
  --user NAME       SSH username (default: current user)
  --remote-path P   Path to caam data on remote (default: same as local)
  --test            Test connectivity after adding
  --no-test         Skip connectivity test
```

Examples:
```bash
# Basic add (auto-detects SSH key)
caam sync add work-laptop 192.168.1.100

# With specific key and test
caam sync add cloud-vm 34.123.45.67 --key ~/.ssh/cloud_key --test

# With port and user in address
caam sync add dev-server jeff@dev.example.com:2222
```

### `caam sync remove`
Remove a machine from the sync pool.

```bash
caam sync remove <name> [flags]

Flags:
  --force    Skip confirmation
```

### `caam sync test`
Test connectivity to one or all machines.

```bash
caam sync test [name] [flags]

Flags:
  --all     Test all machines in pool
```

Output for single machine:
```
Testing connection to work-laptop (192.168.1.100)...
  SSH connection: ✓ OK (latency: 23ms)
  SFTP subsystem: ✓ OK
  CAAM data path: ✓ Found (~/.local/share/caam)
  CAAM version: v1.2.3 (compatible)
  Profiles found: 5
Connection successful!
```

Output for --all:
```
Testing all machines...
  work-laptop:   ✓ OK (23ms, 5 profiles)
  home-desktop:  ✓ OK (45ms, 3 profiles)
  cloud-vm:      ✗ Failed: connection refused

2/3 machines online
```

### `caam sync enable` / `caam sync disable`
Enable or disable auto-sync.

```bash
caam sync enable     # Enable auto-sync after backup/refresh
caam sync disable    # Disable auto-sync
```

### `caam sync log`
Show sync history.

```bash
caam sync log [flags]

Flags:
  --limit N     Show last N entries (default: 20)
  --machine M   Filter by machine
  --provider P  Filter by provider
  --errors      Show only errors
  --json        Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync History (last 20 entries)

TIME                 MACHINE        PROFILE                  ACTION   STATUS
2025-12-19 14:29:00  work-laptop    claude/alice@gmail.com   push     ✓
2025-12-19 14:28:55  work-laptop    codex/work@company.com   pull     ✓
2025-12-19 14:28:50  home-desktop   claude/bob@gmail.com     push     ✓
2025-12-19 12:15:30  cloud-vm       *                        sync     ✗ timeout
...
```

### `caam sync discover`
Discover machines from SSH config.

```bash
caam sync discover [flags]

Flags:
  --add       Add discovered machines to pool
  --test      Test connectivity to discovered machines
```

Output:
```
Discovered machines from ~/.ssh/config:

  NAME           ADDRESS          KEY                   STATUS
  work-laptop    192.168.1.100    ~/.ssh/work_key      not in pool
  home-desktop   10.0.0.50        ~/.ssh/id_ed25519    not in pool
  github         github.com       (skipped: git host)

Add discovered machines to sync pool? (y/N): y

Added 2 machines. Run 'caam sync test --all' to verify connectivity.
```

### `caam sync edit`
Open sync config (CSV file) in editor.

```bash
caam sync edit
```

Opens `~/.caam/sync_machines.csv` in $EDITOR (or sensible default).

### `caam sync queue`
Manage the retry queue.

```bash
caam sync queue [flags]

Flags:
  --clear     Clear all pending retries
  --process   Process pending retries now
  --json      Output as JSON
```

Output:
```
Sync Queue: 2 pending

  PROFILE                  MACHINE       ATTEMPTS   LAST ERROR
  claude/alice@gmail.com   cloud-vm      2          connection refused
  codex/work@company.com   cloud-vm      1          timeout

Use 'caam sync queue --process' to retry now.
Use 'caam sync queue --clear' to clear the queue.
```

## Implementation

### Location
`cmd/caam/cmd/sync.go` (new file)

### Structure
```go
var syncCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "sync",
    Short: "Sync vault across machines",
    Long:  `Synchronize auth profiles with other machines in your sync pool.`,
    RunE:  runSync,
}

var syncInitCmd = &cobra.Command{...}     // First-time setup wizard
var syncStatusCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncAddCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncRemoveCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncTestCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncEnableCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncDisableCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncLogCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncDiscoverCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncEditCmd = &cobra.Command{...}
var syncQueueCmd = &cobra.Command{...}

func init() {
    rootCmd.AddCommand(syncCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncInitCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncStatusCmd)
    syncCmd.AddCommand(syncAddCmd)
    // etc...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `caam sync` syncs with all machines
- [ ] `caam sync --machine` syncs with specific machine
- [ ] `caam sync --dry-run` shows preview
- [ ] `caam sync init` runs first-time setup wizard
- [ ] `caam sync status` shows pool status
- [ ] `caam sync add` adds machine to pool
- [ ] `caam sync remove` removes machine from pool
- [ ] `caam sync test` tests connectivity (single and all)
- [ ] `caam sync enable/disable` toggles auto-sync
- [ ] `caam sync log` shows history with filters
- [ ] `caam sync discover` finds machines from SSH config
- [ ] `caam sync edit` opens CSV in editor
- [ ] `caam sync queue` manages retry queue
- [ ] All commands have help text
- [ ] --json flag works for scriptability
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] Works gracefully when no machines configured

## Files to Create
- `cmd/caam/cmd/sync.go`
- `cmd/caam/cmd/sync_test.go`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)
- Depends on: caam-683 (SSH transport)
- Depends on: caam-npy (sync algorithm)
